transcript: This is the penultimate track race on the penultimate day of athletics here in this wonderful stadium. The United States are the world record holders and the Olympic champions, but they came through a real scare in the heat. The problem came between Alison Felix and English Gardner. Felix finishing that second leg. This is the line-up. USA against Jamaica in six with very very good quartets from Great Britain Trinidad and Nigeria in the mix for the bronze but all of these nations could win a medal because as we have seen and as history has taught us it is so easy for something to go wrong in the 4x1 Alexei Apo has the responsibility of lead leg Brezhnev brings them home Rimean very very experienced she runs the top bend big deep breath the Ukrainian knows she'll have to start very quickly the trinidadians michelle liai who made both finals she goes second kellyanne baptiste and then khalifa saint fort seymour hackett christiania williams finalist in the 100 meters handing over to the woman of the games elaine thompson campbell brown and frher Philip, a great starter, a good performer, indoors then Tatiana Pinto, pass on anchor leg for the Germans. Gloria Asundu to that graceful, powerful athlete, Lesick Okubare, down the back straight straight, Osuzua, bringing them home. Farajac, Alicia George running that top bend, and on the inside, the Americans, probably smarting a little, but they're on that inside lane, they only have themselves to blame, and some would say they were lucky to get a second chance of defending their olympic title usa canada nigeria germany great britain jamaica trinidad ukraine the final of the women's four by 100 meter relay is this destined to be another golden day for the jamaicans or can the americans prove it's they who have the bragging rights for another four years fascinating race an atmosphere crackling with anticipation here huge pride on the line for the two powerhouse nations of sprinting they're cleanly away Bartoletta usually starts very very fast on the inside and she's already up on Jack's Bartoletta with a storming leg let's watch the changeover it looks good from the Americans on the inside to Alison Felix and going for the Jamaicans Elaine Thompson down the back straight this time on the inside it's good from the Americans the Jamaicans trying to run down the Trinidadians outside them Great Britain still in with a shout but it's America who lead from Jamaica they're coming away it's a sizeable lead and what a performance by the Britons fraser price is trying but it's america who have the bragging rights again fraser price has to settle for silver this time and it's a brilliant bronze for great britain their first medal in this event since la in 1984 well they did it by a whisker they were allowed into the final but they absolutely stormed their way to that gold medal with a sensational performance and Fraser Price despite being an individual Olympic champion twice over 100 meters she could do nothing to close that gap and it was Tori Bowie who had the glorious glorious honour of anchoring the Americans to yet another fabulous gold medal. And the reason that they are there is basically because of Brazil. Because the Brazilian runner in the heats got in the way of Alison Felix as she was going to hand over. There's been a lot of talk about whether that was justified or not. But the fact is they were there and when they were there they were always going to be hard to beat and they've run a We'll be right back. to hand over there's been a lot of talk about whether that was justified or not but the fact is they were there and when they were there they were always going to be hard to beat and they've run it brilliantly the changeovers were very slick just slightly sloppy with the last one but once they got it in the hands they were good jamaica was good early on but the first change was a beauty by the americans after bartoletta ran such a good leg and it was fel who put them on the map in the second leg it was a brilliant run from Tiana Bartoletta and then a fantastic bend from English Gardner the Jamaicans had a fabulous quartet but I think the Americans felt as though they had a point to prove there and there was no way shellyanne fraser price was going to be able to close that gap she pulled away from nieta of great britain britain by the way producing a national record 41 77 for a fantastic bronze the trinidadians tried to close in on the germans before but that was academic a great moment for these four women and one that so nearly never came their way in the first place 41-01 for the united states 41-36 for jamaica that national record for great britain in the bronze medal position germany trinidad tobago ukraine canada nigeria the finishing order for the rest of them there they are they must have been breathing a huge sigh of relief they had to run it off last night to get in get in they did and get in all the way to goal well sometimes it's just meant to be but didn't they finish it off in style the united states by some margin are once again the olympic champions silver this time for jamaica and a fabulous bronze for great britain along with a national record as well
